name: Cornish Saffron Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease two 9x5-inch loaf pans.
2. Line a broiler pan with foil.
3. Place saffron on the foil and spread it thinly.
4. Under a very low broiler, gently dry the saffron for 3-4 minutes or until dry.
5. Crush the dried saffron to a fine powder in a small bowl.
6. Add boiling water and let stand for 8 hours.
7. Dissolve 1 teaspoon of sugar in 1/4 cup warm water.
8. In a separate bowl, add yeast and mix with warm water until frothy (10-15 minutes).
9. In a large bowl, combine flour, salt, lard, and butter; cut in finely.
10. Add remaining sugar and currants to the flour mixture and stir.
11. Stir the saffron into the warm water and add it to the flour mixture.
12. Mix in yeast until a dough forms.
13. Lightly knead the dough, cover, and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk.
14. Knead the dough and divide it into two equal parts.
15. Form each part into rectangles with width equal to length of loaf pan.
16. Roll up the dough jellyroll style and place seam side down in greased loaf pans.
17. Press to fill corners. Cover and let stand at room temperature until risen to top of pans.
18. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
19. Bake for 35-45 minutes or until golden brown, covering with foil if necessary to prevent overbrowning.
20. Cool on a wire rack. Let stand for 1 day before serving.
Makes 2 cakes.
